data.title: Luks Group (Vietnam) Anticipates Turnaround to Profit in 2024
data.description:
Luks Group (Vietnam Holdings) expects a net profit between HK$10 million and HK$30 million in 2024, against a net loss of HK$98.7 million in 2023, a Friday bourse filing said.

The cement company attributed the expected turnaround to a decrease in impairment loss on the properties, plants, and equipment of the group's cement business.

Shares of the company closed over 1% higher on Friday.

data.title: TUI Poised to Benefit from Summer Holiday Demand — Market Talk
data.description: TUI's first-quarter update is encouraging and concerns among some investors that holiday demand has plateaued are overdone, HSBC analysts Ali Naqvi and Joseph Thomas say in a research note. Current industry data suggests there remains good momentum for the summer, despite slower booking volumes and cost pressures, the analysts say. 82% of customers are planning to take holidays this year compared with 79% of survey respondents a year ago, the analysts say. "Operators like TUI are making smart decisions by focusing on high volume business and offsetting inflationary pressures by new product initiatives, including their growing mix of dynamically packaged holidays." The analysts raise their target price on TUI shares to 10.90 euros from 10.20 previously. Shares trade 0.69% lower to 7.15 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

data.title: DNO rallies after acquisition of Sval Energi
data.description:
** Shares in DNO are up 7.7% after the Norwegian oil firm announced acquisition of Sval Energi Group for $450 million

** The company says that the transaction will quadruple DNO's North Sea production and lift its total output to around 140,000 boepd

** Pareto, who acts as adviser to DNO in the transaction, highlights the significant cash flow from the Norwegian portfolio following the acquisition, removing the dependence on Kurdistan to finance DNO's dividend

** It expects benefits of around $100 million from liquidity synergies and lower funding costs

** Carnegie analyst Oddvar Bjorgan also notes no auction process as a positive for DNO

** DNO shares are on track for best day since August 2024 and among best performers on STOXX 600 index

data.title: Emirati Stocks Slip as Investors Eye Key US Labor Data
data.description:
Emirati stocks ended the trading week on a downbeat note as traders await key US employment data set for release later in the day.

At Friday's close, the FTSE ADX General Index fell 0.893%, while the DFM General Index was 0.97% in the red.

The US Bureau of Labor Statistics will release fresh data on payrolls, unemployment, and average earnings at 8:30 am local time, which could provide clues for the Federal Reserve's next policy moves.

"The prospect of further Fed relief remains an increased probability in the second half of the year, with market players pricing in 2-3 rate cuts by year-end potentially taking the federal funds rate to a low of 3.75%. The prospect of such action, however, is rooted in fears of emerging weakness in the domestic economy, and specifically, the U.S. labor market, which failed to materialize in the aftermath of earlier rate cuts last year," Stifel said in a note.

Back in the United Arab Emirates, Dubai Electricity and Water Authority (DFM:DEWA) is inviting international developers to express interest in its 1,600-megawatt solar park project in Dubai until March 21. The utility was down 0.784% at closing.

Emirates Integrated Telecommunications (DFM:DU), d/b/a du, joined forces with Microsoft to enhance cybersecurity offerings in the UAE. The partnership will integrate Microsoft Azure's advanced security and artificial intelligence tools into du Tech's managed services portfolio. The telecoms giant's shares fell 1.840%.

Meanwhile, e& UAE, a unit of Emirates Telecommunications Group (ADX:EAND), d/b/a e&, signed an agreement with Samsung Gulf Electronics to develop new connectivity technology, improve device experiences, and advance smart home integration across the UAE. The telecom company closed flat.

data.title: LiveRamp to Reduce Workforce by 5%, Will Incur $6.5 Mln Charge in 4Q
data.description:
By Denny Jacob

LiveRamp will reduce its workforce by about 5%, or 65 employees, as part of a broader effort to boost profitability and efficiency.

The data-software company said friday it will incur about $6.5 million of restructuring and related charges, substantially all of which it expects to incur in the fourth quarter of its fiscal year ending March 31, according to a securities filing.

Write to Denny Jacob at denny.jacob@wsj.com

data.title: AltaGas 4Q Profit Jumps on Strength in Utilities
data.description:
By Dean Seal

AltaGas recorded a more profitable fourth quarter than it did a year earlier on strength in its utilities segment.

The Calgary, Alberta-based energy-infrastructure company posted a profit of 203 million Canadian dollars ($142 million), or C$0.68 a share, compared with C$113 million, or C$0.40 a share, in the comparable period a year earlier.

Normalized earnings came in at C$0.76 a share. Analysts polled by FactSet had been expecting C$0.71 a share.

Revenue for the last three months of 2024 was C$3.26 billion, down from C$3.29 billion a year earlier. Analysts had been expecting C$3.52 billion.

The company's utilities business had a strong year and closed out the quarter with higher earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, its key profitability metric. Ebitda for its midstream business was flat year over year.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.description:

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

data.title: Algonquin Power & Utilities Q4 Adjusted Net Earnings, Revenue Fall
data.description:
Algonquin Power & Utilities reported Q4 adjusted net earnings Friday of $0.06 per share, down from $0.12 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.09.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $584.8 million, compared with $588.2 million a year earlier.

Four analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $634.9 million.

data.title: PFC to issue multiple tenor bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's Power Finance Corp plans to raise 80 billion rupees ($920 million) through sale of multiple maturity bonds, three bankers said on Friday.

The state-run company plans to raise at least 7 billion rupees each through one year and one month, and three year and four month bonds, with a greenshoe option to retain 33 billion rupees for each issue.

The company has invited coupon and commitment bids for the bonds on Thursday, they said.

PFC did not reply to a Reuters email seeking comment.

data.title: GenSight Biologics Completes Equity Raise to Fund General Corporate Needs
data.description:
GenSight Biologics said Friday it raised 900,000 euros from existing investors by issuing 3,829,355 new shares with attached warrants.

The French biopharma company plans to use the proceeds to support general corporate needs as it faces delays in restarting its early access program, which is now expected in April.

data.title: AI Regulation Should Focus on Applications, Mistral AI CEO Says - Market Talk — Market Talk
data.description: Regulation of artificial intelligence should focus on the application of the technology rather than the technology itself, Mistral AI Chief Executive Arthur Mensch said in an interview at the Mobile World Congress in Barcelona. "When you're building a medical diagnostic assistant, you need to make sure that it's accurate enough," Mensch said. However, regulating the models behind the technology itself "makes little sense in that it's more or less a programming language," he adds. Mensch said Mistral AI is engaging with the European Commission, the EU's executive arm, on the application of the EU AI Act. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: Paramount Global's Sale to Skydance Media May be Blocked by Delaware Judge
data.description:
Paramount Global 's $8 billion sale to Skydance Media could be blocked from closing after a Delaware judge agreed to consider a class action lawsuit alleging that the sale short-changes public shareholders, Reuters reported Thursday citing a court filing.

Pension funds for New York City employees that own Paramount stock filed a class action lawsuit in Delaware's Court of Chancery saying that a special committee of Paramount's board did not consider a $13.5 billion proposal to acquire Paramount, according to the report.

Chancellor Kathaleen McCormick agreed to expedite the pension funds' lawsuit but declined to issue a temporary restraining order to block the deal, Reuters said.

Paramount and Skydance did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment on the matter.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)
